topic: Opus 4.6 & Agent Workflow — What Can We Learn to Improve This Workflow

sources.title: Claude Code Docs — Create Custom Subagents
Anthropic Engineering — Effective Harnesses for Long-Running Agents

summary: Custom subagent definitions (.claude/agents/) should replace ephemeral /spawn prompts — highest-ROI improvement
Agent Teams (experimental) introduce mesh-network coordination with shared task lists, replacing manual /sync
Persistent subagent memory enables cross-session learning for code reviewers, debuggers, and security scanners
Effort controls (low/medium/high/max) enable per-command cost optimization — quick tasks should use lower effort
Context compaction reduces /pause+/resume friction for long sessions
Anthropic's multi-agent research shows 90.2% improvement with orchestrator+workers, but at 15x token cost
Long-running agent harness patterns (JSON progress, bootstrapping rituals, git recovery) align with existing workflow
1M context window is powerful but <=3-task plan batching remains valuable as a verification discipline
Skill preloading into subagents maps directly to our existing skills library
Three-phase rollout: agents now, memory next, Agent Teams when stable
recommendation: Phase 1: Create .claude/agents/ definitions for 7 specializations with skill preloading and model routing. Add effort-level guidance to commands. Phase 2: Enable persistent memory for key agents, strengthen /resume with bootstrapping rituals. Phase 3: Integrate Agent Teams when they exit experimental.
